meaning: adj.野蛮的,残暴的,粗野的,(声音)刺耳的,沙哑的
examples: He thought the poetry of Whitman barbarous.
I love to sail forbidden seas, and land on barbarous coasts.
He felt trees, flowers, and fences without purpose were "inartistic if not barbarous."
Here was Bill, heir to Iskander in all of his blond barbarous glory, equal parts courteous and cruel.
They think it is a "barbarous relic, " as John Maynard Keynes described the gold standard, and a superstition rather than an investment.
